$schema_note: Machine-readable traceability matrix for NVIDIA certification coverage in the Aegis-GPU labs. Source of truth for docs/nvidia-exam-coverage.md and frontend/tests/test_exam_coverage.py.
integrity_statement: Objectives are transcribed from the current official NVIDIA certification blueprint page listed below. No exam dumps, leaked questions, or brain-dumps were used. Completing these labs does not guarantee passing the exam.

rating_definitions.Full: Learner can explain the concept (learning guide + observe screenshots), execute it (terminal step with accepted-command validation), and troubleshoot it (a discrete injected-fault step, step.fault === true, in a mapped lab).
rating_definitions.Partial: Explain + execute, but no discrete injected-fault troubleshooting step is present (often because it is hardware/facility limited, or the lab teaches a fix-flow without a flagged fault).
rating_definitions.Theory-only: Explanation and observable reasoning only; no command execution possible in a sandbox.
rating_definitions.Missing: No observable learning outcome in the repository.

certifications.name: NVIDIA-Certified Associate: AI Infrastructure and Operations

objectives.text: Describe AI, machine learning, and deep learning and how they relate
Explain GPU vs CPU architecture and why GPUs suit AI workloads
Distinguish training from inference
Identify the factors driving AI adoption
Describe key AI use cases and the industries applying them
Describe the NVIDIA software stack (CUDA-X, NeMo, NIM, Triton, RAPIDS)
Describe the AI development lifecycle (data prep, train, optimize, deploy, monitor)
Describe NVIDIA AI platforms and solutions (DGX, Base Command, AI Enterprise)
Explain GPU interconnect: NVLink and NVSwitch
Explain multi-GPU communication: NCCL and all-reduce
Describe InfiniBand fabric for AI clusters
Describe RoCE / Ethernet networking for AI
Describe AI storage: parallel filesystems and GPUDirect Storage
Size cluster compute: GPUs per node and per job
Plan facility requirements: power delivery and cooling
Scale with reference architectures: SuperPOD and scalable units
Describe DPU / BlueField offload and DOCA
Compare cloud vs on-prem AI deployment (TCO, control)
Monitor GPU health and telemetry (DCGM, utilization, ECC)
Schedule AI jobs with Slurm and Kubernetes
objectives.validation: terminal-accepted (pending/drain fix-flow, no discrete injected fault)

objectives.text: Virtualize GPUs: MIG, vGPU, and time-slicing
Troubleshoot GPU faults: ECC errors, NVLink degradation, NCCL fallback

hardware_blocked.reason: Real power draw and cooling behavior require physical DGX/HGX hardware and datacenter facility instrumentation. The lab teaches the sizing math and thermal/PDU reasoning against realistic fixtures but cannot measure real kW or rack inlet temperature in a sandbox. Rated Partial.

Cloud vs on-prem procurement and real TCO depend on live vendor pricing and organizational constraints. The lab teaches the decision framework against representative figures; it cannot execute a real purchase or provision cloud capacity. Rated Partial.

MIG and vGPU require supported NVIDIA GPUs plus a licensed vGPU / NVIDIA AI Enterprise stack. The labs use faithful simulated nvidia-smi / mig / vgpu output; real partitioning cannot run without the hardware and license. Simulated to Full.
NVLink/NVSwitch topology requires multi-GPU NVLink hardware; the lab simulates nvidia-smi nvlink output and a degradation fault.
InfiniBand fabric requires real IB switches/HCAs and a subnet manager; the lab simulates ibstat/ibdiagnet output.
Multi-node Slurm and Kubernetes GPU scheduling require a real cluster; the labs simulate srun/sbatch and kubectl output against fixtures. Rated Partial (no discrete injected-fault step).

remaining_enhancements: Add a discrete injected-fault troubleshooting step (step.fault === true) to the storage/gds labs (obj 2.5) — e.g., GDS not engaging and falling back to CPU bounce buffers — to raise it from Partial to Full.
Add a discrete injected-fault troubleshooting step to the slurm/k8s labs (obj 3.2) — e.g., a job stuck Pending on an unsatisfiable GRES/gang request — to raise it from Partial to Full.
Objectives 1.4 and 1.5 remain Theory-only: they are conceptual (adoption drivers, use cases/industries) with no sandbox-executable command.

weighted_scores.method: For each domain, score = domain_weight_pct * (sum of objective rating values / objective count). Total = sum of domain scores.
